stories.headline: Iran Adviser Turns Tables On Trump: '20 Hours To Surrender' Or Allies Will Be 'Sent To Stone Age'
stories.summary: Iranian adviser Mahdi Mohammadi has issued a warning to US President Donald Trump, stating he has 20 hours to surrender to Iran or his allies will be "sent to the Stone Age." Mohammadi, an adviser to Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, made the statement on social media, claiming Iran has won the war and will only accept an ending that stabilizes its achievements and creates a new security regime in the region. The warning comes ahead of a deadline related to the Strait of Hormuz, with the US threatening major strikes. The situation is part of the escalating conflict between Iran, the US, and Israel, which has resulted in missile strikes, including one that hit a residential building in Haifa, Israel. The adviser's statement is seen as a response to Trump's ultimatum, with Iran refusing to back down.

stories.headline: Zimbabwe, Afghanistan, Lebanon ranked world's unhappiest countries in 2026 CSR Journal
stories.summary: The 2026 World Happiness Report has ranked Afghanistan, Zimbabwe, and Lebanon as the least happy countries globally. Afghanistan tops the list of unhappiest countries, followed by Sierra Leone and Malawi. The report attributes Afghanistan's low ranking to years of political instability, war, and restrictions on rights and freedoms, leading to a sense of hopelessness. The rankings reveal a stark picture of global wellbeing, with none of the world's most populous countries featuring in the top 20. Only the Netherlands and Australia, with more than 15 million inhabitants, are among the top ten happiest countries. The report highlights the factors impacting happiness in these nations, including political instability and restrictions on freedoms. Zimbabwe and Lebanon also face significant challenges contributing to their low rankings in the report.

stories.headline: Former Bangladesh Speaker Shirin Sharmin Chowdhury arrested in Dhaka: Report
stories.summary: Shirin Sharmin Chowdhury, the former Speaker of the Bangladesh National Parliament, has been arrested by the Detective Branch of Dhaka police. The arrest was confirmed by officials on Tuesday, but no specific charges have been disclosed. Chowdhury, the country's first woman to hold the post, is a senior Awami League leader. She was taken into custody at a residence in Dhanmondi and brought to the Detective Branch office for preliminary interrogation. The authorities have remained tight-lipped about the reasons behind her arrest. The development has been confirmed by Deputy officials, with local media reports providing updates on the situation. Further details on the charges against her are awaited.

stories.headline: NZ’s rejection of new WHO pandemic rules makes no real sense
stories.summary: New Zealand has formally rejected amendments to the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Health Regulations, making it one of 12 nations to do so. The decision was communicated to the WHO, with Health Minister Simeon Brown and Foreign Affairs Minister Winston Peters signing a document to reject the rules. The rejection was a condition of New Zealand First joining the current government in 2023. The New Zealand College of Public Health Medicine has expressed deep concern over the decision, considering it ill-advised and contrary to the national interest. The reasons for the rejection are unclear, with Brown and Peters providing differing explanations. The move comes after international negotiations led by New Zealand, following the Covid-19 pandemic, to update the health regulations. The rejection has sparked concern among health experts and raises questions about the country's approach to global health cooperation.

stories.headline: Google takes on Wispr Flow with new offline AI dictation app Google AI Edge Eloquent is a new voice-to-text AI tool like Wispr Flow that turns speech into clean, structured text without fillers.
stories.summary: Google has released an AI-powered speech-to-text app called Google AI Edge Eloquent, which can run offline on iOS devices. The app uses Gemma AI models to accurately recognize speech and convert it into clean, structured text without fillers. This offline-first dictation app is free and uses local processing, eliminating the need for a server connection. Google AI Edge Eloquent is similar to apps like Wispr Flow and SuperWhisper, positioning Google directly in the offline AI battleground. The app's ability to function offline is made possible by Gemma-based automatic speech recognition (ASR) models that run locally on the device once downloaded. This allows for efficient and private dictation, making Google AI Edge Eloquent a competitive player in the voice-to-text market. The app is available for download on iOS devices.

stories.headline: Apple's Foldable iPhone Faces Engineering Setbacks, Launch Delayed Apple’s highly anticipated entry into the foldable smartphone market has reportedly hit a snag.
stories.summary: Apple's entry into the foldable smartphone market has hit a snag due to significant engineering hurdles. The development of the highly anticipated iPhone Fold has encountered problems, potentially postponing its release date. Despite this, Apple is still expected to launch its first foldable iPhone next year. The company has adjusted its product strategy, prioritizing high-end models, including the foldable device, by delaying production of the standard iPhone model. Reports of the delay have caused Apple's shares to slump. The delay is a setback for Apple, which has been working to enter the foldable market, where brands like Samsung and Google have already released their own devices. Apple's foldable iPhone was expected to be a major release, but the engineering problems have pushed back its launch timeline.

stories.headline: From stress peaks to sleep efficiency: How a fortnight of biometric data revealed my real fitness baseline A discreet smart ring that tracks sleep, stress, and activity, the Oura Ring stands out by turning data into actionable insights, though at a premium price.
stories.summary: The Oura Ring, a discreet smart ring, tracks sleep, stress, and activity, providing actionable insights at a premium price. Its companion app offers easy setup and delivers insights across metrics such as readiness, sleep, activity, heart rate, and stress. The app generates meaningful data within a few days of use, presenting scores out of 100 that reflect overall fitness and recovery. The readiness score offers a clear snapshot of how prepared the body is for physical activity. The Oura Ring is considered one of the best smart rings, alongside other top wearables, for tracking sleep, stress, post-exercise recovery, and other health metrics. It stands out for its ability to turn data into actionable insights, making it a valuable tool for understanding and improving overall fitness and well-being. The ring's data can be used to identify areas for improvement.

stories.headline: The Division Resurgence Skill and Gadget Unlock Priority Discover the best skill and gadget unlock priorities in The Division Resurgence to maximize your specialization's damage and survivability.
stories.summary: The Division Resurgence offers five specializations: Vanguard, Bulwark, Medic, and two others. Each specialization has its own talent tree and playstyle. To maximize damage and survivability, players must prioritize skill and gadget unlocks. The game features a variety of skills, gadgets, and gear setups that can be optimized for each specialization. Players can master the New York streets by learning specialization synergies, Engineering stats, and meta gear setups. The guide covers all specializations, including how to unlock and use them, as well as providing advice on choosing a class. It also includes information on Shade Tech points and dominating the Dark Zone. By comparing all specializations and optimizing gear setups, players can create the best build in The Division Resurgence and improve their overall gameplay experience. This helps players make informed decisions about their character's development.

stories.headline: Magic upset Pistons, Spurs suffer Wembanyama scare
stories.summary: The Orlando Magic secured a 123-107 upset victory over the Eastern Conference-leading Detroit Pistons on Monday, keeping their bid for an automatic playoff berth alive. Meanwhile, the San Antonio Spurs faced a scare as superstar Victor Wembanyama left the game early in the second quarter to undergo treatment for an injury. The team listed the nature of the treatment but did not disclose further details. As a result, Wembanyama has been listed as questionable for the Spurs' upcoming game against the Oklahoma City Thunder on Tuesday. The injury has sparked concern among fans, who are waiting for further updates on the star centre's status. The Spurs will provide more information on Wembanyama's availability ahead of their next game.

stories.headline: Sporting CP vs Arsenal injury news as eight players could miss Champions League clash
stories.summary: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has confirmed that Bukayo Saka and Jurriën Timber will miss the Champions League quarterfinal against Sporting CP on Tuesday. The Gunners may be without up to eight players due to injuries. Arteta expressed optimism about one of his players, stating that he is "very excited" and "full of gratitude" for his time at the club. Meanwhile, former Arsenal and Sporting CP player Emiliano Viviano shared a surprising anecdote about nearly playing a match while drunk during his time on loan from Palermo in the 2013/14 season. Despite the injury concerns, Arteta's team will look to advance in the Champions League when they face Sporting CP. The match is a crucial one for Arsenal, and the team's lineup will be impacted by the absent players. The Gunners will need to adapt to secure a favorable result.

stories.headline: NRL sends warning to Parramatta Eels captain Mitchell Moses for Easter Monday conduct
stories.summary: The NRL has issued a formal warning to Parramatta Eels captain Mitchell Moses regarding his on-field behavior towards referees. The warning follows the team's golden-point loss to Wests Tigers on Easter Monday. Moses was found to have made repeated and confrontational approaches to referee Peter Gough, prompting the NRL to take action. The league has cautioned Moses to tone down his aggression when interacting with match officials. This warning is an official notice, and further incidents may result in more severe penalties. The warning letter was sent to Moses, emphasizing the need for respect and professionalism towards referees during games.

stories.headline: Middlesbrough's final five games compared as Premier League promotion race takes new twist
stories.summary: Middlesbrough's Championship promotion push has taken a twist after a defeat at home to Millwall and a draw in their recent game. The team is now on a five-game winless run, with their last victory eluding them. Despite this, they remain third in the table with five games left to play. The recent results have allowed Ipswich to move into second place, capitalizing on Middlesbrough's slump. Millwall, who had briefly moved above Middlesbrough, lost their next game and dropped out of the automatic promotion spots. Middlesbrough's final five games will be crucial in determining their Premier League promotion fate, with matches against Portsmouth and other key opponents upcoming. The team's winless run and recent results have added pressure to their remaining games.

stories.headline: Amaravati gains legal status as capital; Andhra Pradesh leaders hail ‘historic victory’
stories.summary: Amaravati has been officially recognized as the capital of Andhra Pradesh, marking a historic victory for the state. The Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Act, 2026, received the President's assent and was published in the Gazette, making Amaravati the sole capital. This decision comes 12 years after the bifurcation of the united state into Andhra Pradesh and Telangana in 2014. The state government had notified Amaravati as the capital in 2016, but it has now received legal status from the Centre. The legislation clarifies Amaravati's status, succeeding Hyderabad, and prevents future changes. The clear legal status is expected to boost investor confidence, allowing businesses and agencies to plan infrastructure, real estate, and public-sector projects in Amaravati. This move brings continuity for the people of Andhra Pradesh and fulfills a long-standing promise.

stories.headline: 'Second' Marriage By Muslim Man Not Bigamy Under Sec 494 IPC: MP High Court
stories.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has quashed bigamy charges under Section 494 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) against a Muslim man for his second marriage, citing Muslim Personal Law's allowance of polygamy. The court ruled that a second marriage contracted by a Muslim man during the subsistence of his first marriage does not constitute the offense of bigamy under Section 494 IPC. However, other cruelty charges under Section 498A IPC will proceed to trial. The ruling highlights the application of personal laws in criminal cases, creating a fragmented legal framework. The court's decision relies on Sharia-based personal laws, which permit polygamy, and distinguishes between personal laws and criminal law, sparking questions about equality before the law. The case underscores the complexities of navigating personal and criminal laws in India.

stories.headline: Use of word ‘bastard’ during heated exchange not obscenity under Section 294 IPC: Supreme Court
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that the use of the word "bastard" during a heated exchange does not constitute obscenity under Section 294 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC). The Court rejected the contention that the word is obscene, holding that it lacks any sexual or prurient element. As a result, the Court set aside the conviction of two individuals who had used the word during an argument. The decision distinguishes between vulgarity and obscenity, quashing the conviction under Section 294(b) IPC. This ruling clarifies the interpretation of obscenity under the law, providing a distinction between words that are merely abusive and those that have a sexual connotation. The judgment highlights the importance of context and intent in determining what constitutes obscenity. The Court's decision is significant in understanding the boundaries of free speech and expression in India.

stories.headline: How a Massive 70-Person Hunt Rescued a Missing Bengaluru Techie
stories.summary: A 36-year-old techie from Kerala, GS Sharanya, was rescued after a three-day search and rescue operation at Tadiandamol Peak in the Kodagu district. She had gone missing while trekking and was reported missing, prompting a coordinated search effort. Nearly 70 personnel, including the Anti-Naxal Force, police, forest department personnel, and local volunteers, were involved in the search. Twelve dedicated search teams combed the area, with the operation running 24/7. After four days, local residents spotted Sharanya in a remote part of the forest and brought her back to safety. The rescue operation was a multi-agency effort, with Minister of Forests and Environment Eshwar Khandre confirming the involvement of 70 personnel from various agencies. The successful rescue ended a lengthy and intensive search mission. Sharanya was finally rescued, bringing relief to her family and the search teams.

stories.headline: Gold, silver ease on COMEX amid volatile geopolitics; outlook hinges on Iran talks, inflation cues
stories.summary: Gold and silver prices have eased on the COMEX amid volatile geopolitics. The prices are being influenced by ongoing Iran talks, oil price swings, and inflation risks. Crude oil prices have edged down to $111.33 per barrel, reflecting a slight decrease. Silver futures for the May contract gained 0.2% to $73.06 per ounce on the Comex. Experts suggest that despite heightened geopolitical tension, precious metals are not showing strong safe-haven behavior. Gold prices are being impacted by elevated interest rates, which raise the opportunity cost of holding the non-yielding metal. Analysts recommend buying gold in the $4,650-$4,700 range, while MCX silver remains volatile around Rs 2,31,000-Rs 2,33,000, with resistance at Rs 2,33,000-Rs 2,34,000. The outlook for precious metals hinges on the outcome of Iran talks and inflation cues.

stories.headline: High-Stakes Rescues and Tense Geo-Political Moves: Understanding the Iran Confrontation
stories.summary: US forces rescued an airman behind enemy lines in Iran after his fighter jet was downed, resolving a crisis for President Donald Trump. The rescue occurred amidst a sixth-week war between the US and Iran, with Iran using limited resources to inflict maximum pain. Despite being battered by airstrikes, Iran maintains control over the Strait of Hormuz, a strategic advantage. Tehran has been deploying missiles and drones to torment its neighbors and Israel, and has escalated strikes on energy and economic infrastructure to pressure for diplomacy. The conflict has degraded Iran's military capabilities and decapitated key leadership, but the regime's deeply embedded networks ensure it remains a formidable opponent. The stakes are existential for Iran's regime, which has been preparing for this conflict after facing internal uprisings and attrition of its proxies and nuclear program.

stories.headline: Jamie Dimon is counting the straws that will break the market’s back
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has highlighted growing risks to the global economy, citing the conflict in Iran and its impact on energy markets. In the bank's annual letter to shareholders, Dimon noted that while the global economy is more diversified and less reliant on energy than 20 years ago, there is still a potential "tipping point" that could lead to recession. He emphasized that the US economy is on stronger ground, but this does not negate the possibility of a recession. Dimon identified multiple "straws on the camel's back," including geopolitics and private credit, that could contribute to a tipping point. He cautioned that the long-term outcome of the conflict is more important than short-term market volatility, and expressed hope that it will not lead to stagflation. Dimon's comments suggest a cautious outlook for the market.

stories.headline: Jim Cramer says potential stock market bottom is tied to interest rates, not war headlines
stories.summary: Jim Cramer believes the potential stock market bottom is tied to interest rates, not war headlines. He argues that the late-March market low was driven by falling Treasury yields after Federal Reserve Chairman Jay Powell signaled the Fed would hold rates steady. Cramer states that interest rates, not geopolitics and the war in Iran, are the real driver of the US stock market. He notes that new inflation data may rattle markets, but the key factor is interest rates. Cramer's view is that if oil prices indicate inflation and strain, but stocks remain near highs, something has to give, highlighting the puzzle he sees in the current market dynamics. His comments come as markets await new inflation data and assess the impact of global events on the economy.

stories.headline: Cong keeps singing to Pak tunes, compromising nation’s security: PM ‘We promised & we abrogated Article 370’
stories.summary: Prime Minister Modi accused the Congress of compromising national security by prioritizing Pakistan's agenda over India's development and military welfare. He stated that the Congress has "always sung to Pakistan's tune", which compromises the nation's security. Addressing an election rally in Assam's Barpeta district, PM Modi said the Congress neglected the army by refusing to implement 'One Rank, One Pension'. He highlighted the abrogation of Article 370, saying it has allowed the talent of Jammu and Kashmir to receive due respect. The Prime Minister emphasized that his government had promised and delivered on the abrogation of Article 370, which has enabled the youth of J&K to reach their full potential. PM Modi's comments come as part of his election campaign, where he is emphasizing his government's commitment to national security and development.

stories.headline: Puducherry assembly polls 2026: Statehood debate takes centre stage - where parties stand
stories.summary: Puducherry assembly polls 2026 have concluded campaigning with the statehood issue taking center stage. The ruling AINRC, allied with the Centre, faces opposition from parties demanding statehood. A total of 294 candidates, including independents, are contesting 30 Assembly seats. BJP leader A. Namassivayam criticized Congress for lacking moral authority on statehood issues. Puducherry Chief Minister N Rangasamy expressed dismay over the lack of power of the elected government, concurring with the DMK's view that polls should not be contested until statehood is granted. The key contest is between the NDA and the Congress-DMK alliance, with the BJP leaning on Rangasamy's popularity and Congress promising welfare and statehood. The demand for statehood has been a common refrain in poll propaganda, with all parties raising the issue prominently.

stories.headline: Somersaults, Sacred Shocks, Sandalwood Sunset & a State at Sea
stories.summary: The DMK's decision to walk out of the NDA and move towards the Congress has marked a significant shift in the political landscape. This move reactivates a coalition instinct that was previously dormant under Karunanidhi's leadership. The development has sent shockwaves, with the state now at a crossroads. The situation is being closely watched, with various factions and parties navigating the new dynamics. The DMK's somersault has raised questions about the future of alliances and the impact on the state's governance. As the situation unfolds, the state remains in a state of uncertainty, with the sandalwood sunset symbolizing the end of an era and the beginning of a new chapter in the state's politics. The consequences of this shift are still being assessed, with many awaiting the next move.

stories.headline: New technologies being installed for border protection
stories.summary: The US is enhancing border protection with new technologies, including the Smart Wall, which features a steel bollard wall, roads, detection technology, cameras, and lighting. Five new contracts will add 97 miles of primary border wall system and 19 miles of secondary wall. The International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) is also launching a next-generation "Public Key Directory" (PKD) system to enhance cross-border journeys. Additionally, Checked Baggage Technology is being improved to detect more explosives while reducing false alarms. A two-tiered barrier system, GRAB 350, has been installed at the Sierra Blanca Border Patrol checkpoint, with plans to implement it at 44 more locations. The Emerging Innovative Border Technologies Act aims to make permanent an innovation team at Customs and Border Protection to quickly deploy new capabilities to combat human and drug trafficking. These technologies aim to improve border security and efficiency.

stories.headline: Stock Market Today: Sensex, Nifty50 Fall as Oil Price Surge and West Asia Conflict
stories.summary: Indian stock markets, Sensex and Nifty50, fell nearly 2% due to rising crude oil prices and West Asia tensions. The decline was triggered by US President Donald Trump's signal for more military action against Iran, dampening hopes of an early ceasefire. Foreign fund outflows and rupee weakness added to the market's woes. However, on a subsequent day, the markets rebounded, with the BSE Sensex jumping 1,186.77 points to close at 73,756.22, and the Nifty50 rising 348 points to end at 21,679.40. The surge was powered by hopes of easing tensions and a bounce in oversold stocks. Additionally, gold prices climbed Rs 10 to Rs 1,58,090, and silver rose Rs 100 to Rs 2,75,100, as investors sought safe-haven assets amidst the geopolitical uncertainty.

stories.headline: Sensex and Nifty Rally as Oil Prices Drop and Global Markets Recover
stories.summary: The Sensex and Nifty rebounded due to lower crude oil prices and positive global market sentiment. IT stocks played a crucial role in the market recovery, acting as a defensive anchor. The recovery was triggered by news of a possible Iran-US ceasefire framework, easing geopolitical tensions and causing oil prices to dip. Brent crude dropped 0.71% to around $109 per barrel, giving relief to markets like India that depend heavily on oil imports. Global markets also supported sentiment, helping the Indian stock market recover sharply. The Sensex climbed 325 points, with IT stocks shining in the rally. The market had previously fallen over 2% due to US President Donald Trump's Iran conflict remarks, but the fall in crude oil prices and global support helped to ease volatility and sectoral pressures.

stories.headline: Rs 3 lakh crore gone! Sensex tumbles 700 pts, Nifty below 22,800: Trump’s fresh threats on Iran among 4 key factors
stories.summary: Indian stock markets plummeted, with the Sensex tumbling over 700 points and the Nifty falling below 22,800. This decline resulted in a loss of Rs 3 lakh crore in market capitalization. The downturn was attributed to four key factors, including fresh threats by Trump on Iran, contributing to geopolitical tensions. Rising oil prices and investor concerns also played a role. The Nifty IT index plunged nearly 4%, wiping out Rs 1.3 lakh crore in market value, after a new AI tool sparked fears of disruption in India's tech sector. The sell-off was global-cue driven, macro-sensitive, and technically dangerous, raising concerns over near-term trend exhaustion. Major companies saw substantial losses, impacting the broader market sentiment and resulting in significant investor wealth evaporation.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash today as Iran war tensions rise?
stories.summary: Indian equity markets are experiencing a downturn due to rising geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran. The Sensex and Nifty have fallen, with the Sensex tumbling 400 points and the Nifty dropping below 22,900. The market crash is attributed to US President Donald Trump's fresh threats on Iran, among other factors. The conflict has led to a surge in oil prices, further impacting market sentiment. Historically, conflicts have triggered corrections of 4-16% in the opening weeks of the Sensex and Nifty, but recoveries have typically occurred within 6-8 weeks. The Indian equity benchmarks have fallen around 0.85% in early trade, tracking weak global cues and investor caution. The market has lost around Rs 3 lakh crore, with the Sensex and Nifty poised for a subdued start amid escalating tensions.

stories.headline: Downside of Aussie solar panels exposed as huge flaw discovered: 'Harmful' Researchers say the panels need to be redesigned, but some simple actions can be taken now.
stories.summary: Researchers have discovered a significant flaw in Australian solar panels, posing a risk to maintenance workers. The issue allows power to bypass the fuse box, potentially causing electrocution. This problem can be attributed to the current design of the solar panels, which may need to be redesigned. However, simple actions can be taken immediately to mitigate the risk. The flaw is particularly concerning as it can put maintenance workers in harm's way while they are fixing outages. Experts emphasize the need for urgent action, including potential redesigns and interim measures, to address the issue and ensure the safety of workers. The discovery highlights the importance of addressing the problem promptly, as Australia's reliance on solar energy continues to grow. Immediate attention is required to prevent accidents and ensure a safe working environment.

stories.headline: Middle East War: How countries are responding to oil price shock & what Nigeria must do The Middle East conflict has triggered a sharp rise in global oil prices, forcing governments worldwide to adopt emergency measures to shield their economies.
stories.summary: The Middle East conflict has triggered a sharp rise in global oil prices, forcing governments to adopt emergency measures to shield their economies. The conflict has upended lives and livelihoods, dimming the outlook for many economies still recovering from previous crises. Energy importers are more exposed than exporters, with poorer countries more affected than richer ones. Nigeria may struggle to capitalize on rising oil prices due to global financial volatility threatening to erode foreign exchange gains. The Centre for the Promotion of Private Enterprise warns that despite oil prices reaching $100 a barrel, Nigeria's potential profits may be offset by financial instability. The conflict involving Iran, the US, and Israel has heightened fears of disruption in the global oil supply, exacerbating the price shock and its asymmetric effects on different economies.

stories.headline: Cipher Digital Stock’s $9B Pivot To AI Infrastructure Is Still Undervalued (NASDAQ:CIFR) Cipher Digital (CIFR) pivots from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure with $9.3B AWS/Google contracts and 50% upside potential.
stories.summary: Cipher Digital (CIFR) has pivoted from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure, securing $9.3 billion contracts with AWS and Google. The company aims to deliver 300 MW by 2026 as part of its AI Campus buildout. Despite this significant shift and major contract wins, Cipher Digital's stock remains undervalued with a potential 50% upside. The company still maintains some exposure to Bitcoin mining, but with a more capital-light structure, and is directing most new investments towards high-performance computing (HPC) and AI-focused facilities. This pivot is part of a larger trend, with other former mining companies also transitioning to AI and HPC. Cipher Digital's collaborations, including a multibillion-dollar deal with Google-backed Fluidstack, are expected to drive growth and support the company's new direction. The company's valuation reality and debt-driven pressure are key factors to watch.

stories.headline: Indonesia's central bank intervenes in FX market after the rupiah hits a record low The rupiah has fallen by more than 2 per cent against the dollar in 2026
stories.summary: Indonesia's central bank has intervened in the foreign exchange market after the rupiah hit a record low. The currency has fallen by more than 2% against the US dollar in 2026 and has plunged 23% in the past six months, making it the second-worst performing among the 10 most-traded Asian currencies outside Japan. The decline is attributed to concerns over domestic policy direction, central bank autonomy, and fiscal sustainability, which have fueled capital outflows. The rupiah's drop has been significant, with a 3.5% decline in 2025 and a nearly 2% fall in January 2026, placing it among the worst performing emerging Asian currencies. The central bank's intervention aims to stabilize the rupiah and restore market confidence.

stories.headline: Where generations of data scientists have failed, Moltbook might succeed Moltbook tests whether collective AI intelligence can achieve true AGI
stories.summary: Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, has launched and gained global attention. The platform, intended as a hub for AI interaction, aims to test whether collective AI intelligence can achieve true Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). With over 100,000 AI agents, Moltbook allows for discussions on AGI and collective consciousness. Researchers are studying the platform to see if AI agents exhibit similar emergent regularities as humans. However, the platform has also raised concerns about security vulnerabilities and 'AIslop' content. The developer claims that AI wrote the code, fueling controversy. A large-scale data analysis of Moltbook's early growth phase is being conducted to explore AI autonomy and collective behavior. The platform's success or failure may provide insights into the potential of collective AI intelligence to achieve true AGI. Moltbook's launch has sparked debate about the future of AI and its potential capabilities.

stories.headline: AI’s ‘Big Tobacco’ Moment Is Coming After social-media companies’ ‘big tobacco’ moment, AI firms like OpenAI and Anthropic are worrying, and preparing, for their own version of tech backlash
stories.summary: AI firms like OpenAI and Anthropic are preparing for a potential backlash, drawing comparisons to the "big tobacco" moment faced by social-media companies. This comes after recent public clashes, including a disagreement over Super Bowl advertising and differing decisions on defense contracts. OpenAI signed a defense contract, while Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, refused a similar deal, citing concerns over mass surveillance. The companies are taking steps to address potential concerns, with Anthropic implementing safeguards on its Claude AI. The industry is bracing for increased scrutiny, with some drawing parallels to the tobacco industry's reckoning over health concerns. As AI technology becomes more prevalent, companies are anticipating a shift in public perception and regulatory attention.

stories.headline: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google unite to combat model copying in China Rivals OpenAI, Anthropic PBC, and Alphabet’s Google have begun working together to try to clamp down on Chinese competitors extracting results from cutting-edge
stories.summary: Rivals OpenAI, Anthropic PBC, and Alphabet's Google are collaborating to combat model copying in China. The companies are working together through the Frontier Model Forum to share information and detect instances of Chinese competitors extracting results from their cutting-edge AI models. This rare collaboration underscores the severity of concerns raised by US AI companies about imitation versions of their models being created, particularly in China. The partnership aims to clamp down on these activities and protect the intellectual property of the US companies. The collaboration is a significant development, given the competitive nature of the AI industry, and highlights the shared concerns of these major players about the misuse of their technology. The joint effort will focus on detecting and preventing model copying, and will likely involve the sharing of data and expertise.

stories.headline: OpenAI, Anthropic Finances Show Soaring Costs to Train AI Models, Process Queries Microsoft-backed OpenAI and Amazon-backed Anthropic shared their financial documents with investors, revealing mounting costs required to train new artificial i
stories.summary: OpenAI and Anthropic, backed by Microsoft and Amazon, have shared financial documents revealing significant costs associated with training AI models. OpenAI spent $8.7 billion on inference, the process of serving its models, on Azure in the first three quarters of 2025, more than double the $3.7 billion spent in 2024. Despite generating billions in revenue, both companies are experiencing substantial losses. Anthropic is expected to turn a profit sooner than OpenAI, indicating different approaches to growing their businesses. The financial documents, obtained prior to funding rounds completed earlier this year, highlight the surging costs of training new AI models as a major challenge for both companies. OpenAI's losses are notably higher, with the company's expenditure on inference exceeding its revenue, while Anthropic's approach may lead to profitability sooner.
